text.title: reranking-models Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for reranking-models as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Comparing relevance scores across different reranking providers (e.g., Cohere 0.9 vs local cross-encoder 0.9).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reranker output scores are not calibrated probabilities. The same numeric value from two providers carries different semantics. Use rankings, not absolute scores, for cross-provider comparisons.

failure-mode.description: Relying on reranking to surface a document not retrieved in the first stage.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Rerankers only re-order the candidate set returned by ANN. If the relevant document is not in the top-50 candidates, reranking cannot surface it.

failure-mode.description: Cohere API returns results indexed into the original documents list — assuming results are in input order.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents must preserve the original list order and use result.index to map back, not assume results are in input order.

failure-mode.description: LLM-based reranking with JSON output returning indices out of range if the LLM hallucinates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents must bounds-check all rankings values before indexing into the candidate list.
